changelog: 2026-05-17: Added 62 features from devil's-advocate audit (docs/10-gap-analysis.md) and social brainstorm (docs/11-social-features.md). New categories: onboarding, grace, lifecycle, recovery, safety, couples, family, b2b, infrastructure, creator_economy. Social tier expanded from 8 → 40 features. Corrected stale total_features count (was 135 claimed, actual was 124).

2026-05-17: Added 79 features from app-landscape research (docs/12-app-landscape.md) prompting the shift from launcher → life OS. New categories: integration, fitness, mind, nutrition, sleep, finance_v2, location, substance, personal_crm, life_os, reading, identity. Total 124 → 186 → 265.

phases.mvp: Core launcher + basic restrictions + device admin (4-6 weeks)
phases.v1: Full restriction engine + tracks + productivity system (3-4 months)
phases.v2: Social/group features + AI + financial stakes (6+ months)
phases.v3: Advanced AI + community + companion devices (12+ months)

features.name: Custom Launcher Home Screen
features.description: Replace default home screen. Shows only: projects, todos, behavior indicator, 5 widgets max, 5 app dock.
features.notes: Foundation for everything. Uses android.intent.category.HOME intent.

features.name: 5-App Dock (2 Fixed + 3 User)
features.description: Max 5 visible apps. Phone + Messages fixed. 3 configurable with 24hr change cooldown.
features.notes: Simple constraint. No app grid. Must type to find hidden apps.

features.name: Hidden App Search (No Drawer)
features.description: No browseable app drawer. Swipe up for search bar. Must type app name. Friction before launch.
features.notes: Eliminates 'just browsing' behavior. Must know what you want.

features.name: App Usage Tracking
features.description: Track time per app, unlock count, session duration, foreground detection.
features.notes: UsageStatsManager API. User grants in Settings. Foundation for all restriction logic.

features.name: Widget System (Max 5 Our Widgets)
features.description: 5 widget slots on home screen. Only FocusLauncher widgets (project, todos, behavior, streak, timer).
features.notes: No third-party widgets (distractions). 24hr cooldown on rearranging.

features.name: The Lobby (Wait Timer)
features.description: Mandatory wait screen before distraction app opens. 15s productive apps, 90s distractions. No skip.
features.notes: Highest impact-to-effort ratio. Most impulses die in 15 seconds.

features.name: Cognitive Tax (Puzzles Before App)
features.description: Solve scaling puzzle to open distraction. 1st open=swipe, 5th=multiplication, 10th=haiku.
features.notes: Scales with daily opens. Resets at midnight. AI-validated haiku at high tiers.

features.name: Progressive Dimming Overlay
features.description: Overlay dims screen 2%/min in distraction apps. Near-black at 25min.
features.notes: Uses SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW overlay. Not actual brightness change — overlay-based dimming.

features.name: Variable Ratio Punishment
features.description: Restrictions are RANDOM. Sometimes distraction app works, sometimes locked for no reason.
features.notes: Unpredictability breaks habit loops. Same psych as slot machines, reversed.

features.name: The Boredom Preservatory
features.description: 2-min intervals of NOTHING. Blank screen. No apps. Train boredom tolerance.
features.notes: Triggers between sessions or after excessive unlocks.

features.name: Intent Declaration
features.description: Must state what you'll do + set timer before opening distraction. Fail to finish? App locked for day.
features.notes: Pairs with Lobby. During wait, type your intention.

features.name: Escalating Lockout
features.description: Open blocked app → locked out of EVERYTHING. 5min→30min→2hrs escalation per violation.
features.notes: Overlay-based lockout. Only Phone+Messages bypass.

features.name: Gatekeeper Tasks
features.description: Must complete boring task (math problem, Duolingo lesson equivalent) before accessing addictive apps.
features.notes: Built-in mini-challenges. Not third-party dependency.

features.name: Time Debt System
features.description: Overspent today? Double penalty locked out tomorrow. Debt compounds daily.
features.notes: Persistent state. Debt tracked in DB. Resets only when paid off.

features.name: Context-Aware Locks (GPS+Time+Calendar)
features.description: At gym=only music. At desk 9-5=only work apps. In bed after 10pm=only kindle/meditation.
features.notes: Geofencing API + Calendar read + time rules. Battery consideration for GPS.

features.name: Dream Mode (Night Wind-Down)
features.description: After 10pm, phone fades to: tomorrow's schedule, quote, breathing exercise. All apps disappear.
features.notes: Time-triggered UI swap. Gentle transition animation.

features.name: One App At A Time (No Multitask)
features.description: No app switcher. 3-second close animation before next app opens.
features.notes: Can disable recent apps via Accessibility. Force single-task.

features.name: App Roulette Lock
features.description: Pick 3 apps to restrict. System randomly picks ONE to be available each day.
features.notes: Simple random selection at midnight. Fun unpredictability.

features.name: Weather-Gated Apps
features.description: Beautiful day outside? Entertainment locked. Only unlocks in bad weather.
features.notes: Weather API call. Simple threshold logic.

features.name: Reverse Notifications (Scheduled Check-Ins)
features.description: No push notifications. Manual check-in at 2-3 scheduled windows per day only.
features.notes: NotificationListenerService auto-dismisses. Stores and re-delivers at windows.

features.name: The Replacement Engine
features.description: Intercept distraction app open. Offer: 'Read 2 pages? 10 pushups? Write 3 sentences?' Accept = earn time.
features.notes: Shows during Lobby wait. Converts wasted impulse into micro-action.

features.name: The Paradox Lock
features.description: Must write 50-word essay on why you SHOULDN'T open app. AI judges. Good=locked. Bad=also locked.
features.notes: Humorous but effective. On-device AI or template matching.

features.name: Choice Overload Weapon
features.description: Want blocked app? Presented with 47 alternative activities in chaotic grid. Decision fatigue → give up.
features.notes: Psychological exploit. Most people just close.

features.name: Sound Diet
features.description: Track audio consumption hours. After threshold: forced silence. No media playback.
features.notes: Can track MediaSession state but not total audio accurately. Partial implementation.

features.name: Per-App Network Blocking (VPN)
features.description: Cut internet to specific apps via local VPN. Instagram loads but shows nothing new.
features.notes: VPNService + UID-based filtering. Proven approach (NetGuard model).

features.name: Bandwidth Throttling
features.description: Don't block — throttle to 1kbps. Videos won't load. Images take 30s. Text-only experience.
features.notes: Extension of VPN approach. Rate-limit rather than block.

features.name: Mantra Gate (Voice Unlock)
features.description: Speak personal mantra 5 times verified by speech recognition before phone unlocks.
features.notes: Android SpeechRecognizer (offline). Anti-replay via utterance variance. Whisper mode for public.

features.name: Mantra Scaling by Track Level
features.description: Level 1-2: 1 rep. Level 3-4: 3 reps. Level 5+: 5 reps. Level 8+: mantra + state today's task.
features.notes: Simple config tied to track level.

features.name: Whisper Mode / Type Fallback
features.description: For public settings: whisper detection or type mantra 5 times as alternative.
features.notes: Accessibility requirement. Can't force voice-only.

features.name: VIP Contact List (Max 10)
features.description: Up to 10 numbers that ALWAYS ring through regardless of focus mode. 24hr cooldown on changes.
features.notes: Critical for safety. Phone must not be dangerous. Uses READ_PHONE_STATE + contacts.

features.name: Emergency Pass System (5/week)
features.description: 5 weekly passes for non-VIP calls to ring through. Resets Monday. Repeat caller (2x/3min) = free override.
features.notes: Pass counter in local DB. Repeat-caller detection via call log.

features.name: Hover Window for Calls
features.description: Floating overlay during focus showing caller name + answer/decline. Doesn't break focus context.
features.notes: SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW. Shows on top of any screen.

features.name: Auto-Reply on Decline
features.description: Decline call → auto-sends pre-written SMS: 'In focus mode. Will call back in X min.'
features.notes: SEND_SMS permission. User configures templates.

features.name: Silent Mode Enforcement
features.description: Phone in silent/DND by default during focus. Only VIP/pass calls break silence.
features.notes: ACCESS_NOTIFICATION_POLICY. Programmatic DND toggle.

features.name: Todo System with App-Unlock Rewards
features.description: Create todos. Tag which app unlocks after completion. Verification: none/photo/timer/location/companion.
features.notes: Core productivity loop. Max 3 app-unlocking todos/day.

features.name: Project Tracking (Max 3 Active)
features.description: Create projects with tasks, milestones, progress bar. Main home screen element. Streak per project.
features.notes: Projects visible EVERY unlock. Progress = completed tasks / total.

features.name: Behavior Indicator (5-State Meter)
features.description: Real-time indicator: Thriving→Neutral→Drifting→Sinking→Drowning. Based on session/unlocks/app type/time.
features.notes: Algorithm combines multiple signals. Color tints home screen.

features.name: Journal / Day Summary (Auto-Generated)
features.description: Nightly auto-summary: focus score, todos done, project progress, patterns, AI insight. User adds mood/note.
features.notes: Local data compilation. AI insight via on-device or cloud LLM.

features.name: Focus Timer (Pomodoro Built-In)
features.description: 25min focus blocks as widget. Between blocks: brief app access window. Phone IS the timer.
features.notes: Widget on home screen. Auto-starts DND during focus block.

features.name: Face-Down Detection
features.description: Proximity sensor detects phone face-down. Tracks duration. Rewards intentional breaks.
features.notes: Proximity sensor + accelerometer. No permission needed. Free hardware sensors.

features.name: Morning Routine Sequencer
features.description: First 60min after alarm: one task at a time from morning routine. Can't access anything else until done/skipped.
features.notes: Detects alarm dismiss as trigger. Sequential task display.

features.name: Voice-to-Todo Capture
features.description: Double-tap power button → 5sec voice → AI converts to todo + assigns to project.
features.notes: Power button shortcut may conflict with OEMs. Alternative: notification action button.

features.name: Idea Parking Lot
features.description: Gesture captures screenshot + note while in distraction app. Review later, convert to project tasks.
features.notes: Transforms consumption into creation seeds.

features.name: Effort Estimation Training
features.description: Estimate task time on creation. Log actual after. Track estimation accuracy over weeks.
features.notes: Simple time logging + statistical comparison.

features.name: Input/Output Ratio Tracker
features.description: Track consumption (reading/watching) vs creation (writing/coding/designing). Show ratio on home screen.
features.notes: Categorize apps as input/output. UsageStats per category.

features.name: Shutdown Ritual
features.description: At end-of-day time: prompt for what you shipped + tomorrow's #1 priority. Then enter evening mode.
features.notes: Time-triggered prompt. Stores reflection in journal.

features.name: The One Thing (Daily Priority Pin)
features.description: Force-declare ONE most important task each morning. Pinned at top ALL day. Reminded every 2hrs.
features.notes: Simple pinning + periodic notification. High impact for trivial effort.

features.name: Energy Zones
features.description: User tags energy pattern (high/med/low by time of day). Phone adapts: high=deep work only. Low=lighter tasks.
features.notes: User-configured schedule. Rules engine applies different restriction sets per zone.

features.name: Time Blocking Enforcer (Calendar Integration)
features.description: Calendar says 'Deep Work 9-11am' → phone ENFORCES it. Not suggestion. Apps locked.
features.notes: READ_CALENDAR. Match event titles to restriction profiles.

features.name: Track System (Gradual Commitment Scaling)
features.description: Create tracks with 10 levels. Start tiny, scale on proven consistency. 7-day promotion threshold.
features.notes: Core differentiator. Prevents burnout. Progressive overload for discipline.

features.name: Level-Up / Demotion Mechanics
features.description: 7 consecutive success days = level up. 3 fail days = drop one level (with grace period).
features.notes: Demotion framed as calibration not failure. Faster re-promotion on second attempt.

features.name: Multiple Independent Tracks
features.description: Separate tracks for: Deep Work, Sleep, Morning Routine, Fitness, Learning. Each levels independently.
features.notes: Different life areas, different progression speeds.

features.name: Smart Starting Point (Baseline Detection)
features.description: Observe usage 3 days before suggesting Level 1. Never require >25% behavior change from baseline.
features.notes: 3-day observation period. Sets achievable Level 1 based on actual usage.

features.name: Sad Self Notifications (Personified Regret)
features.description: During distraction use, receive notifications from 'Sad [Name]' — future self that loses when you scroll.
features.notes: References specific projects/todos. Escalating intensity. Max 1 per 5 min.

features.name: Voice Style Selection (4 Personalities)
features.description: Choose: Gentle & Disappointed, Blunt & Frustrated, Sarcastic & Tired, Philosophical & Reflective.
features.notes: Different template sets per voice. User picks during setup.

features.name: Celebration Self (Proud [Name])
features.description: Flip side: when doing well, same character sends proud/encouraging messages.
features.notes: Same system, positive templates. Makes sad version hit harder by contrast.

features.name: AI-Generated Messages (On-Device LLM)
features.description: Move from templates to on-device AI generating contextual messages. More personal, less repetitive.
features.notes: Gemma Nano or Phi-3 on-device. Falls back to templates if device can't run.

features.name: Adaptive Frequency (Back-Off on Ignore)
features.description: If user ignores 5 messages in a row across sessions, Sad Self gets quieter. One powerful message max.
features.notes: Prevents learned helplessness / notification fatigue. Tracks response rate.

features.name: Identity Voting (Consumer vs Creator)
features.description: Every app open framed as vote: 'This is a vote for: [Consumer] or [Creator]?' Single tap.
features.notes: Atomic Habits logic. Minimal UI. Huge psychological reframe.

features.name: Session Receipts
features.description: After closing ANY app: full-screen receipt showing time spent + equivalents (meals cooked, km run, etc).
features.notes: Accessibility detects app switch → overlay. Sub-second response.

features.name: Legacy Counter (Lifetime Stats)
features.description: Home widget: 'Entertainment: 4,247hrs. Creation: 312hrs. Ratio: 13.6:1.' Lifetime accumulation.
features.notes: Cumulative stats from UsageStats. Can import historical data on first install.

features.name: Builder/Consumer Mode Toggle
features.description: Two modes. Builder: unlimited productive apps. Consumer: daily budget for entertainment. Identity split.
features.notes: Simple mode toggle with different rule sets. Visual indicator of current mode.

features.name: The Mirror Widget (Camera as Wallpaper)
features.description: Front camera as home screen background. See YOUR face every unlock. Reduces mindless behavior 40%.
features.notes: CameraX preview as background. CAMERA permission. Optional feature.

features.name: Future Self Video (Pre-Recorded Deterrent)
features.description: Record video during strong moment. Plays before every relapse. Can't skip.
features.notes: Record during onboarding. Store locally. Play as intercept before blocked apps.

features.name: Opportunity Cost Ticker (Overlay)
features.description: Persistent overlay in distraction apps: 'This session = $34.50 at your hourly rate.'
features.notes: User sets hourly rate once. Overlay calculates in real-time. Small floating widget.

features.name: Intervention Screen (14 Unlocks)
features.description: Full-screen after excessive unlocks: 'This is attempt #14. Your phone is not your friend.'
features.notes: Simple counter → threshold → overlay. Trivial to implement, high impact.

features.name: Deathbed Simulator (Lifespan Projection)
features.description: Monthly: 'At current rate: 11.3 YEARS on social media by end of life.' Projected to remaining lifespan.
features.notes: Simple math: daily average × estimated remaining days. Devastating number.

features.name: The Last Day Test
features.description: 'If you die tomorrow, would your last 30 min be this?' Yes/No before distraction app.
features.notes: One-screen prompt during Lobby. Simple but existentially heavy.

features.name: Phantom Vibration Counter
features.description: Detects phone checks without notification. 'You checked for no reason 34 times today.'
features.notes: Compare unlock events vs pending notification count. Unlock with 0 notifications = phantom check.

features.name: Pattern Detection & Insights
features.description: '38 of 47 failures between 2-4pm. Always after Sarah's notification.' AI finds your weakness patterns.
features.notes: Statistical analysis of usage data. Time correlations, app sequences, notification triggers.

features.name: Anchoring Attack
features.description: Before showing your stats, show 'most disciplined user this week: 12min total.' Your 4hrs feels massive.
features.notes: Requires backend for community comparison. Or use static aspirational numbers.

features.name: App Tombstones
features.description: Killed/deleted apps show gravestones on home: 'Here lies Candy Crush. Age: 47 plays.'
features.notes: Fun visual. Detect PACKAGE_REMOVED broadcast. Display memorial.

features.name: Future Self Messenger (Scheduled Letters)
features.description: Write letters to yourself that deliver at random future weak moments. Past-you coaching present-you.
features.notes: Stored locally. Delivered when behavior indicator hits Yellow/Red.

features.name: Group Creation & Management
features.description: Create accountability groups. Invite friends. Shared streaks, voting, visibility.
features.notes: Backend required (Firebase/Supabase). Auth + real-time sync + push notifications.

features.name: Group Uninstall Approval (Voting)
features.description: Can't uninstall without group members voting to allow it. Majority required.
features.notes: Core differentiator. In-app deactivation flow requires group approval before proceeding.

features.name: Shame Notification to Friends
features.description: Fail → silent notification to friend: 'Risky opened TikTok 8th time today.'
features.notes: Push notification via backend. User opts into which failures are shared.

features.name: Team Streaks
features.description: Squad of 5. All must stay installed. You quit = everyone's streak dies.
features.notes: Shared streak counter. Backend sync. Push alerts on threat.

features.name: The Disappointment API
features.description: One person you respect gets ONE notification/week: your worst stat only.
features.notes: Weekly cron → compute worst stat → push to designated person. Simple but devastating.

features.name: Co-Working Presence (Live Focus Status)
features.description: See when group members are in focus mode. Tiny avatars with green dots on home screen.
features.notes: Real-time presence via WebSocket/Firebase Realtime DB.

features.name: Group Sprints
features.description: Schedule 2hr sprint with group. Everyone commits to a task. Real-time progress. Celebrate at end.
features.notes: Scheduled event + progress sync + completion celebration.

features.name: Sponsor System (AA-Style)
features.description: Designated person gets notified on uninstall attempts. Can call you. Human intervention.
features.notes: Push notification + optional auto-dial. Powerful but requires willing sponsor.

features.name: Device Admin Registration
features.description: Register as Device Administrator. Greys out uninstall button. 6-7 step journey to deactivate.
features.notes: Single most effective anti-uninstall measure. Standard API.

features.name: In-App Deactivation Flow (72hr Cooldown)
features.description: Must use app's own UI to begin uninstall. 72-hour countdown with daily check-ins.
features.notes: App controls deactivation experience. Shows stats, plays future self video, etc.

features.name: Nuclear Passphrase
features.description: 20+ char passphrase set on install. Written on paper. Stored inconveniently. Required to begin uninstall.
features.notes: Physical separation of key from device. Forces intentional retrieval.

features.name: Future Self Video on Uninstall Attempt
features.description: Pre-recorded 'don't quit' video plays full-length, can't skip, before uninstall proceeds.
features.notes: Reuses future self video. Plays during 72hr cooldown check-ins too.

features.name: Public Shame Post on Uninstall
features.description: Uninstall triggers auto-post to community/friends: '[Name] quit after X days.'
features.notes: Posted to group feed. Opt-in during onboarding when user is rational.

features.name: Relapse Prediction on Uninstall Screen
features.description: '94% of users who uninstalled at day 12 reinstalled within 9 days. Save yourself the spiral?'
features.notes: Requires aggregate data from user base. Or use convincing static stats initially.

features.name: Graduated Freedom (Reduces Need to Uninstall)
features.description: Restrictions ease naturally over time. By Level 10, app is near-invisible. No reason to remove.
features.notes: Best anti-uninstall: make the app not annoying at mastery. Inherent to track system.

features.name: The Applause (Positive Reinforcement for Stopping)
features.description: Put phone DOWN after short use → speaker plays applause sound. Pavlov for quitting.
features.notes: Detect lock event after <3min session. Play sound. Simple and delightful.

features.name: Compound Time Bank
features.description: Every avoided minute banks with compound interest. Monthly 'earnings' report of saved time.
features.notes: Difference between limit and actual usage = banked. Compound formula applied.

features.name: Focus Score Economy (Earn/Spend Points)
features.description: Focus Points as currency. Earn by productivity. Spend to unlock apps. Phone runs on productivity fuel.
features.notes: Economy balancing needed. Prevent inflation. Ties into todo-unlock system.

features.name: Skill Tree Phone (Unlock Features by Real Actions)
features.description: Fresh install = calls only. Real-world tasks unlock phone features. 7-day gym streak = Spotify.
features.notes: Extreme mode. Phone is EARNED. Requires robust verification system.

features.name: Productivity Heatmap Widget
features.description: GitHub-contributions-style heatmap showing hourly productivity. Green=focused. Red=wasted.
features.notes: Visual widget. Weekly grid. Instant pattern recognition.

features.name: Streak System (Multi-Domain)
features.description: Separate streaks for: focus, sleep, creation, todos completed. Each with own counter and best record.
features.notes: Multiple independent streaks. Home screen shows primary streak.

features.name: Earned Pixels (Home Screen Beauty as Reward)
features.description: Home screen starts 1-bit grayscale. Focused hours earn color/resolution. Beauty = discipline.
features.notes: Visual theming based on focus score. Degraded = ugly. Thriving = beautiful.

features.name: Money Stake (Escrow Deposit)
features.description: Deposit $50-500. Fail commitment = donated to cause you hate. Complete = refund + bonus.
features.notes: Stripe/payment integration. Legal complexity per jurisdiction. High impact when available.

features.name: Streak Insurance Value Display
features.description: Show monetary value of streak on uninstall screen. 'Your streak = $94 in rewards. Forfeit?'
features.notes: Can be virtual currency or tied to real discounts. Loss aversion trigger.

features.name: Physical NFC Key Unlock
features.description: Distraction apps only unlock when phone touches NFC tag on desk. Forces physical journey to location.
features.notes: Standard NFC permission. User buys cheap NFC sticker. Tag ID stored.

features.name: Breath to Unlock (4-7-8 Pattern)
features.description: Breathing pattern verified by mic timing. 16 seconds minimum. Calms nervous system before access.
features.notes: Mic detects exhale/inhale timing, not speech. Privacy-friendly. Works offline.

features.name: WearOS Companion (Heart Rate + Vibration)
features.description: Watch app reads HR, sends somatic vibration warnings, detects stress. Feeds data to phone.
features.notes: Separate WearOS app. Health Connect API. Adds biometric layer.

features.name: Posture Lock (Accelerometer)
features.description: Detect lying-in-bed or toilet angles. Block entertainment in those positions.
features.notes: Accelerometer is permission-free. Angle detection is simple math. False positives possible.

features.name: Kazoo Mode (Public Embarrassment Sound)
features.description: Speaker plays kazoo in restricted apps. Can't mute. Public embarrassment as deterrent.
features.notes: Notification sound channel bypasses mute. Fun easter egg for extreme mode.

features.name: The Narrator (TTS Narrates Your Actions)
features.description: Text-to-speech reads aloud: 'He opened Twitter. Again. The 14th time.' From speaker.
features.notes: Android TTS engine. Contextual script generation. Opt-in only.

features.name: Time Dilation UI (Clock Runs 3x Fast)
features.description: Clock in distraction apps runs 3x speed. 5 'minutes' feels like 15. Psychological exit pressure.
features.notes: Custom timer overlay. Not system clock (impossible). Floating widget shows accelerated time.

features.name: Voice Authenticity Gate (Unsayable Confession)
features.description: Must say aloud: 'I am choosing distraction over my goals.' Designed to be psychologically impossible to say sincerely.
features.notes: Uses same speech recognition infra as Mantra. Different intent: shame vs affirmation.

features.name: The Confession Call (Auto-Dial Random Contact)
features.description: After 30min in distraction app, auto-dials random contact. Explain what you're doing or hang up.
features.notes: CALL_PHONE permission. Extremely aggressive. Opt-in extreme mode only.

features.name: Modify Other Apps' Internal UI (Slow Scroll, Pixelate)
features.description: Change scroll speed, pixelate content, distort faces, change animations inside other apps.
features.impossible_reason: Android app sandbox. Cannot inject into another app's rendering pipeline.
features.workaround: Draw overlay ON TOP via SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW. Apply grayscale filter overlay, dimming overlay, or visual noise overlay. Can't modify what's underneath but can make it progressively unpleasant to look at.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 70% — overlay achieves similar psychological effect without actual UI modification.

features.name: Truly Prevent Uninstallation
features.description: Make it literally impossible for user to remove the app under any circumstances.
features.impossible_reason: User always has: ADB uninstall, factory reset, Safe Mode boot. Android gives users ultimate control.
features.workaround: Device Admin (greys out button) + 72hr in-app cooldown + social voting + financial stakes + future self video. Chain of 5-7 barriers. Impulse can't survive the full chain. 95%+ retention vs truly impossible.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 90% — the layered approach stops virtually all impulse uninstalls. Only determined, rational users get through.

features.name: Read Other Apps' Internal Data/Content
features.description: Know exactly what user watched on YouTube, which posts they read, what messages they received inside other apps.
features.impossible_reason: App sandbox prevents cross-app data access. Cannot read another app's memory or database.
features.workaround: 1) Accessibility reads visible screen text (partial). 2) NotificationListener reads notification content. 3) VPN sees DNS queries (which domains, not what content). 4) OAuth API access (YouTube/Reddit/Instagram APIs) for watch/read history with user consent. 5) Built-in browser for web services gives full URL visibility.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 50% — can infer PATTERNS but not specific content without user-consented API access.

features.name: Replace System Clock in Status Bar
features.description: Show 'hours alive remaining' or 'time wasted' instead of actual time in status bar.
features.impossible_reason: Status bar is system UI. Third-party apps cannot modify any part of it.
features.workaround: 1) Since WE are the launcher, our home screen shows our own clock (no status bar needed). 2) Persistent notification with custom time. 3) Widget showing alternative time metrics. 4) WearOS watch face with 'real clock.'
features.workaround_effectiveness: 80% — user sees our clock every time they go home. Only misses it while inside other apps.

features.name: Force-Close/Kill Foreground Apps
features.description: Terminate a running app's process while it's in foreground. True force-stop.
features.impossible_reason: forceStopPackage requires FORCE_STOP_PACKAGES (system-only permission). killBackgroundProcesses only kills background.
features.workaround: 1) Accessibility performs GLOBAL_ACTION_HOME (sends user home). 2) Full-screen immovable overlay makes app unusable (still running but invisible). 3) Launch our activity with NEW_TASK flag to pull user out. 4) DevicePolicyManager.clearApplicationUserData() nukes app data (extreme). 5) App pinning locks to OUR app.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 85% — overlay blockade + home action achieves same result. User can't USE the app even if it's technically running.

features.name: Detect Pupil Dilation / Micro-Expressions
features.description: Use front camera to detect pupil dilation (dopamine response) or emotional state from facial expressions.
features.impossible_reason: Phone cameras lack IR sensors for reliable pupil tracking. ML-based emotion detection is unreliable (60-70% accuracy at best). Battery-destroying constant processing.
features.workaround: 1) WearOS heart rate as proxy for arousal/excitement. 2) Typing pattern analysis (speed, errors = anxiety proxy). 3) Usage pattern as emotional proxy (rapid unlocks = anxious, 3am scrolling = can't sleep). 4) Self-reported mood check-ins.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 40% — behavioral proxies are less accurate than biometrics but still detect patterns.

features.name: Modify Scroll Behavior in Other Apps
features.description: Make infinite scroll finite. Force feeds to end after 20 posts. Reduce scroll speed.
features.impossible_reason: Cannot inject into other app's UI layer. RecyclerView/scroll controllers are private.
features.workaround: 1) VPN blocks CDN after N minutes (no new content loads, scroll shows blanks). 2) Overlay progressively covers more screen area. 3) Accessibility counts scroll events → trigger intervention after threshold. 4) Custom browser with injected JS for web-based services. 5) Network throttle makes loading so slow that infinite scroll becomes finite naturally.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 60% — VPN content cut is closest to 'ending the feed.' User sees stale content but technically CAN scroll.

features.name: Silently Reinstall Self After Uninstall
features.description: If user uninstalls, have a background service that reinstalls the app automatically.
features.impossible_reason: Silent install requires Device Owner (enterprise MDM provisioning). Regular apps cannot install anything without user clicking through system installer UI.
features.workaround: 1) Companion app detects uninstall (PACKAGE_REMOVED broadcast) → nags user to reinstall + notifies group. 2) Backend detects silence (device stops checking in) → sends push to group 'user may have uninstalled.' 3) Device Owner provisioning for 'dedicated focus phone' setup (factory reset + QR code).
features.workaround_effectiveness: 50% — can't reinstall but CAN make the social/financial consequences of uninstalling painful enough to prevent it.

features.name: Intercept App Launch from OUTSIDE Our Launcher
features.description: If user launches app from notification, widget, or another app — intercept BEFORE it opens.
features.impossible_reason: No 'app launch interceptor' API exists. Can only detect AFTER an app has started.
features.workaround: 1) WE are the launcher — all home screen launches go through us (covers 80% of cases). 2) Accessibility detects new window → overlay in 50-200ms (user sees brief flash of app). 3) UsageStats polling (1-3sec interval) + overlay (slower, user sees app briefly). 4) NotificationListener can dismiss notifications before user taps them (prevents notification-triggered launches).
features.workaround_effectiveness: 75% — Accessibility provides near-instant overlay. Most users won't notice the 100ms gap.

features.name: Control System DND/Focus for Other Apps Selectively
features.description: Block notifications from Instagram but allow WhatsApp, without user manually configuring system DND.
features.impossible_reason: Cannot prevent notification GENERATION by other apps. Can only react after notification is posted. Sound/vibration of original may already fire before we can dismiss.
features.workaround: 1) NotificationListenerService instantly dismisses notifications from blocked apps (sub-100ms, usually before sound plays). 2) Full DND mode + whitelist only our app's notifications (re-deliver important ones ourselves). 3) Store dismissed notifications → batch re-deliver at scheduled times. 4) Modify notification channels guidance for user to manually mute specific app channels.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 80% — NotificationListener dismiss is fast enough that most notifications never reach user awareness.

features.name: Persist System Settings Against User Override
features.description: Set grayscale/DND/brightness and PREVENT user from changing it back in system settings.
features.impossible_reason: Cannot lock system settings. User can always navigate to Settings and change anything. No API to prevent settings changes.
features.workaround: 1) Monitor + re-apply loop: background service watches setting changes → re-applies within 1 second. User plays whack-a-mole but service is faster. 2) Overlay-based alternatives: instead of system grayscale, draw desaturating overlay (user can't 'undo' in settings because it's not a setting). 3) Accessibility can detect when user navigates to specific Settings screen → show deterrent overlay.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 70% — monitor+re-apply creates 'it fights back' feeling. Overlay-based approaches give 100% control since they bypass system settings entirely.

features.name: Block App Installation (Prevent Bypass Tools)
features.description: Prevent user from installing other launchers, VPN bypass tools, or screen time disablers.
features.impossible_reason: Cannot prevent installation of apps. PACKAGE_ADDED is a notification AFTER install, not a blocker.
features.workaround: 1) Detect new installs via broadcast → notify group: 'User installed Screen Time Bypass Tool 🚨.' Social shame. 2) Device Owner mode CAN whitelist/blacklist apps (requires enterprise provisioning). 3) Overlay on Play Store showing guilt message when opened. 4) Detect launcher change attempt → alert immediately.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 40% — can't prevent but can make it socially visible. Device Owner mode is 100% effective but requires extreme setup.

features.name: Run Continuously in Background on iOS
features.description: Persistent background monitoring of app usage on iOS like we can on Android.
features.impossible_reason: iOS kills background processes after ~30 seconds. No persistent monitoring. Background fetch is unreliable (15min+, OS decides). Fundamentally hostile to this use case.
features.workaround: 1) FamilyControls/Screen Time API (iOS 15+) — Apple's official framework for app monitoring/blocking. Requires Apple approval + DeviceActivity framework. 2) Shortcuts automations ('When I open X') — user-configured, fragile, limited. 3) Widget-only experience showing stats (refreshes periodically). 4) Accept iOS version is fundamentally limited compared to Android.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 30% — FamilyControls is the only real path on iOS but Apple gates approval heavily and capabilities are limited vs Android.

features.name: Replace Home Screen on iOS
features.description: Custom launcher replacing iOS home screen like we do on Android.
features.impossible_reason: iOS does not allow third-party launchers. Home screen is Apple-only.
features.workaround: 1) Widget-based home screen experience (large widget showing projects/todos). 2) Lock screen widget (iOS 16+). 3) Focus Modes that hide app pages. 4) Shortcuts automations for app-open triggers. 5) Accept that iOS version is a companion app, not a launcher.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 25% — fundamentally different product on iOS. Android IS the platform for this concept.

features.name: Detect Exact Content Consumed (Videos Watched, Posts Read)
features.description: Know specifically which YouTube videos, Reddit threads, Instagram posts the user consumed.
features.impossible_reason: App sandbox prevents reading other apps' internal state. Watch history, read state, etc. are private to each app.
features.workaround: 1) OAuth API integration: YouTube Data API, Reddit API, etc. — user grants access, we pull actual history. Most accurate but requires per-platform integration. 2) VPN DNS/SNI logging shows domains accessed (youtube.com/watch?v=X visible in DNS). 3) Built-in browser for web services gives full URL+content visibility. 4) Accessibility screen reading for on-screen text (rough, battery-heavy).
features.workaround_effectiveness: 65% — OAuth API access gives accurate data for platforms that support it. VPN gives domain-level visibility.

features.name: Play Audio OVER Other Apps Reliably
features.description: Mix our audio (narration, kazoo, shame sounds) at full volume simultaneously with other app audio.
features.impossible_reason: Audio focus system means only one app 'owns' audio at a time. Can request transient focus but other app ducks/pauses.
features.workaround: 1) Notification sound channel — plays regardless of focus (short sounds only). 2) USAGE_ALARM audio attribute — system prioritizes over media. 3) Request AUDIOFOCUS_GAIN_TRANSIENT_MAY_DUCK — other app reduces volume, ours plays, then releases. 4) Media button event to PAUSE other app → play our audio → release. All work on Android with minor limitations.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 85% — notification/alarm channels guarantee our sounds are heard. Can't truly MIX but can interrupt effectively.

features.name: Algorithm/Feed Manipulation Exposure
features.description: Show WHY specific content was shown to user. Expose recommendation algorithm reasoning.
features.impossible_reason: Algorithms are server-side, proprietary, and opaque. No API exposes recommendation reasoning. Would require reverse-engineering each platform.
features.workaround: 1) GENERIC education: show dark pattern taxonomy overlay ('This is an infinite scroll designed to...') rather than specific algorithmic reasoning. 2) Usage-based inference: 'You lingered on 3 similar posts this week — algorithm is exploiting this pattern.' 3) Research-based facts: 'Social media algorithms optimize for engagement, not your wellbeing. Average user is shown X manipulative patterns per session.'
features.workaround_effectiveness: 30% — can't show ACTUAL algorithm but can educate about manipulation tactics generically.

features.name: Heartrate/Stress Gate from Phone Only (No Watch)
features.description: Detect elevated heart rate or stress purely from the phone's built-in sensors without a smartwatch.
features.impossible_reason: PPG (camera-based HR) requires finger on camera + flash — not passive. No stress sensor in phones. Front camera emotion detection is unreliable and battery-killing.
features.workaround: 1) WearOS/Apple Watch companion — REAL HR sensors, passive, continuous. This is the actual path. 2) Typing cadence analysis as stress proxy (fast/erratic = anxious). 3) Unlock frequency as arousal proxy (10 unlocks in 5min = agitated). 4) Time-of-day + calendar stress prediction (historically stressful periods). 5) User self-report: quick mood check-in 2x/day.
features.workaround_effectiveness: 45% — smartwatch gives real biometrics. Behavioral proxies are less accurate but zero-hardware-cost.

features.name: First-Run Sequencer
features.description: Guided 20-min first session: permissions, mantra capture, future-self video, VIP contacts, partner invite, 3-day baseline starts.
features.notes: Single most important UX. Drives day-1 retention. Designed to feel like a ritual, not a settings menu. Gap surfaced in devil's-advocate audit (see docs/10-gap-analysis.md §A1).

features.name: Why Are You Here? Capture
features.description: First screen asks user to type/record WHY they want this — the pain. Stored verbatim. Resurfaced at every weak moment + uninstall attempt.
features.notes: Anchor for everything else. Trivial to build, devastating to ignore.

features.name: Grace Day System
features.description: 1-2 declared grace days per month with zero penalty. Must be declared in advance (not retroactive). Streaks pause, no demotion.
features.notes: Prevents uninstall during life crises (sick, grief, travel, period). System absorbs the shock instead of breaking. Gap §A2.

features.name: Streak Freeze (Earnable)
features.description: Earn 1 streak freeze per 30 days of perfect adherence. Auto-applies on a missed day. Max 3 in reserve.
features.notes: Duolingo-proven mechanic. Without this, 200-day streaks die on one bad day → catastrophic loss aversion → uninstall. Gap §A3.

features.name: Compassionate Demotion
features.description: When dropping a track level, frame as 'recalibration' not 'failure'. Show recovery plan. Faster re-promotion on second climb.
features.notes: Extends TRACK-002. Language and visual tone matter as much as mechanics.

features.name: Graduate Mode
features.description: After 18+ months of mastery, app becomes near-invisible. Stats-only widget, no restrictions, monthly check-in. Optional re-engage if relapse detected.
features.notes: Best anti-uninstall: stop being annoying once you've done your job. Counter-intuitive but ethically required. Gap §A4.

features.name: After-Fall Ritual
features.description: After a streak break: 3-step ritual — short reflection, recommit smaller scope, optional message to accountability partner. No shame, just structure back up.
features.notes: Falling is inevitable. The rebound mechanic is what separates this from punitive apps. Gap §A5.

features.name: Account Recovery via Trusted Contacts
features.description: Lost phone / forgotten passphrase? 2-of-3 trusted contacts vouch via their app to restore your account.
features.notes: Social-graph-based recovery. Inspired by signal/social-recovery wallets. Gap §D6.

features.name: Crisis Detection & Soften Mode
features.description: Detect concerning patterns (3am scrolling >2 weeks + zero todos + low motion + flat mood pings). Auto-soften: Sad Self pauses, gentle check-in surfaces, suggest professional resources.
features.notes: Ethical + legal must-have. A depressed user reading 'future-you is disappointed' 8x/day is a lawsuit. Surfaces crisis hotlines locale-aware. Gap §A6, §D2.

features.name: Manipulation Transparency Page
features.description: Settings page lists every psychological technique the app uses on you (variable ratio, Sad Self, Anchoring Attack, etc.) with toggle to disable each.
features.notes: Required for ethical consistency: we are dark-patterning the user 'for their own good' (RESTRICT-004, PSYCH-013, etc.). Must be opt-out-able. Gap §D3.

features.name: Accessibility Pass
features.description: Audio alternatives for visual overlays (TalkBack-friendly). Type/typing-pattern alternatives to voice mantra. Switch-control compatible. High-contrast theme.
features.notes: Blind/deaf/motor-impaired users currently invisible in spec. Legal requirement in many jurisdictions. Gap §C5.

features.name: Nourishing Consumption Category
features.description: Third category beyond Productive/Distraction: Nourishing (Kindle, audiobooks, documentaries, Duolingo). Encouraged, not blocked. Counts toward 'good consumption' stat.
features.notes: Fixes binary input/output blindness in PROD-011. Reading a book ≠ scrolling TikTok. Gap §B1.

features.name: Mood Pings (2x Daily Emotion Check)
features.description: Two 5-second mood check-ins per day (e.g., 11am + 8pm). Single emoji tap + optional 1-word note. Paired with usage stats for pattern detection.
features.notes: Most valuable insight isn't 'you scrolled 4hrs' — it's 'you scrolled 4hrs every time you felt anxious'. Trivial to build, huge analytical payoff. Feeds SAFETY-001 and PSYCH-012. Gap §B2.

features.name: Cycle / Energy-Window Awareness
features.description: Optional menstrual cycle / energy pattern tracking. Adjusts expectations, lightens restrictions during low-energy phases. User-private.
features.notes: ~50% of users have a cycle that changes focus capacity dramatically. Ignoring it is engineering blindness. Strict on-device storage. Gap §B3.

features.name: Companion Verification for Todos
features.description: Designated friend confirms task completion via push notification. 1-tap 'verified' or 'nope, try again'. Prevents fake-todo gaming.
features.notes: Plugs the 'brush teeth → unlock TikTok' loophole in PROD-001. Strongest verification option. Gap §D5.

features.name: Life-State Mode Set
features.description: Predefined temporary modes: Commute, Travel, Sick, Vacation, Weekend. Each loosens or tightens a specific rule subset. Auto-detected or manually toggled.
features.notes: Extends context-aware locks. Vacation should not = relapse spiral, sick day should not = punishment. Gap §B4.

features.name: Lite Mode for Distraction Apps
features.description: Open Instagram in text-only / grayscale / no-Reels mode. Better-than-all-or-nothing. Implemented via VPN content stripping + overlay.
features.notes: Per-platform implementation challenge. Reels-stripping for IG is achievable via VPN URL filtering. Gap §B5.

features.name: Topic-Level Filter
features.description: Twitter for tech, not politics. LLM classifies content on-device; hides off-topic posts. User defines whitelist topics per app.
features.notes: Hard to implement cleanly (accessibility-read + LLM classify + overlay-hide). Best done via VPN+custom browser for web. Gap §B6.

features.name: Partner Pair Mode
features.description: Pair with romantic partner. Shared streak (e.g., 'both off phones at dinner: 47 days'), shared time-saved counter, shared phone-Sabbath days, 'I'm proud of you' one-tap pings.
features.notes: Huge underserved segment. Different dynamics than 'group of friends'. Gap §C1.

features.name: Phone Sabbath Together
features.description: Schedule a shared full-day disconnect with partner / friends. Both phones go grayscale and lock at agreed time. Reunion summary 24hrs later.
features.notes: Real-world ritual with digital scaffolding.

features.name: Parent/Child Pair Mode
features.description: Parent's app shows child's phone stats + can approve/deny apps. Child's app has age-appropriate UI (no Sad Self, gentler restrictions).
features.notes: Most-installed reason for any focus app. Big market gap. Requires Android Family Link integration consideration. Gap §C2.

features.name: Welfare Check (Idle Detection for Elderly)
features.description: If paired family member has zero phone activity for 12+ hours during their normal active window, ping designated contact. Privacy-preserving.
features.notes: Adjacent market: elderly care. No content shared, only activity heartbeat.

features.name: Therapist / Coach Dashboard
features.description: Web dashboard for licensed coaches/therapists. With client consent, view anonymized patterns, weak hours, mood ↔ usage correlations. Subscription product.
features.notes: B2B SaaS layer. Real revenue source. ADHD coaches will pay. Gap §C3.

features.name: Team Focus Dashboard
features.description: Company-level: managers see team's aggregate focus hours (no individual surveillance). Encourages collective deep-work norms.
features.notes: Anti-surveillance design: only aggregates, no per-user reporting up. Sells to dev shops, agencies. Gap §C4.

features.name: Encrypted Cloud Backup & Device Migration
features.description: End-to-end encrypted backup of all journal/streak/track/social data. Restore on new device with passphrase + recovery contacts.
features.notes: Without this, a new phone = lose 400-day streak. Show-stopping omission in original spec. Gap §D7.

features.name: Data Export (PDF / CSV / Photo Book)
features.description: Export journal as PDF, stats as CSV, year heatmap as image. Optional print-ready photo-book format.
features.notes: GDPR + user trust. Also feeds MERCH-001. Gap §D8.

features.name: Web / Desktop Companion
features.description: Web app for read-only stats, journal, todos, project tracking. Edit-only for journal entries. Phone remains source-of-truth for restrictions.
features.notes: Most knowledge work happens on a laptop. Data dying at phone boundary is a UX gap. Gap §E7.

features.name: Print Shop (Heatmap Poster / Year Journal / NFC Puck)
features.description: Order physical artifacts of your year: heatmap poster, bound journal, branded NFC desk-puck for HARDWARE-001. Real anchor in world.
features.notes: Print-on-demand integration (Printful or similar). Ego-anchoring + revenue. Gap §E2, §E6.

features.name: Track Marketplace
features.description: Verified creators publish curated tracks (e.g., 'Cal Newport's Deep Work Track'). Users browse, clone, follow creator's stats. Revenue split.
features.notes: Creator economy on top of discipline. Powerful flywheel. Gap §E3.

features.name: Mentor Subscriptions
features.description: Pay verified Built user $5/mo for unlocked stat-sharing + DM access. Twitter Subscriptions for discipline mentors.
features.notes: Revenue + mentorship in one product surface.

features.name: Public Builder Profile
features.description: Shareable profile URL (focuslauncher.app/<handle>): streaks, projects shipped (data-verified), heatmap, badges. Default private; user opts in to public.
features.notes: LinkedIn for makers of their own life. External value for internal discipline. Top-3 highest-leverage social feature.

features.name: Built Wrapped (Year in Review)
features.description: Annual recap: projects shipped, hours saved, top distraction defeated, longest streak, top accountability partner. Designed for screen-share to IG/Twitter.
features.notes: Spotify-Wrapped-grade viral surface. Drives massive install spikes annually. We already have all the data. Top-3 highest-leverage.

features.name: Built Verification Badge
features.description: Earned after 365 days continuous use + 3+ shipped projects + 3 community vouches. Visible checkmark on profile. Scarce by design.
features.notes: Scarcity is the whole point. Don't dilute. Once it means something, profile + marketplace + mentor features all gain weight. Top-3 highest-leverage.

features.name: Receipt Wall
features.description: Every time user puts phone down within 3 min, a small tile is added to profile's wall. Visual texture of restraint.
features.notes: Skyline of small wins. Quiet flex.

features.name: Anti-Bio
features.description: Profile bio field describes what user has chosen NOT to do, not what they do. '47 days TikTok-free. Quit Twitter in March.'
features.notes: Reframes identity around subtraction. Small UI affordance, huge tone.

features.name: Profile Highlight Reels
features.description: Pinnable permanent stories on profile (shipped projects, longest streaks, best months). Auto-verified from data.
features.notes: Instagram Highlights, but for discipline. Auto-generated cards, user picks which to pin.

features.name: Aura Avatar
features.description: In-app avatar visibly shifts based on behavior meter: gold ring when Thriving, foggy when Drowning. Friends see your aura on their feed.
features.notes: Stylized, not photoreal. Cute, low-shame visual signal.

features.name: Chronological Feed (No Algorithm)
features.description: Followed-users only, reverse-chronological. No 'suggested', no 'explore'. Brag about this in marketing.
features.notes: Whole point of this app: refuse engagement algorithms. Sacred space.

features.name: Today's Top Builders Tab
features.description: Discover surface sorted by data-verified output (projects shipped, hours focused) not by likes/engagement.
features.notes: Anti-influencer Explore. Output-first ranking.

features.name: Anti-Influencer Tab
features.description: Verified creators publish actual focus rules + real (not curated) stats. 'Follow Tim Ferriss's exact week.' Their numbers compete with yours.
features.notes: Forced honesty for influencers. Their published routines must match their data.

features.name: Hashtag Tracks
features.description: Public joinable tracks: #75hard, #buildinpublic, #nomorningphone. Leaderboards within tag. Thousands of strangers, same protocol.
features.notes: Virality via tags. Lower-friction than friend invites.

features.name: Disappointment Inbox
features.description: Dedicated feed surface showing only Sad Self messages + accountability partners' disappointment notes. Dark-humor scroll of negative reinforcement.
features.notes: Inverts addictive scroll. Strong opt-in only — pair with SAFETY-001.

features.name: Focus Stories (24hr Ephemeral)
features.description: Auto-generated end-of-day card: today's focus hours, todos shipped, streaks. Friends see for 24hr then it's gone. No filters — generated from data.
features.notes: Instagram Stories format, but the content is verified. Low-shame share.

features.name: Mood Voice Notes
features.description: 30-second audio note about today (Clubhouse-style) for close-friends list. Expires in 24hr. Less performative than video.
features.notes: Audio is intimate without being a production. Optional companion to Focus Stories.

features.name: Confession Booth
features.description: Anonymous post of today's failure to community. Strangers send 'I've been there' stickers. ML-curated for genuineness, anti-spam.
features.notes: Therapeutic. Anonymity layer + moderation cost. Pair with SAFETY-001 to soften during crisis.

features.name: Vault Stories (Close Friends)
features.description: Share REAL stats — weak hours, embarrassing scrolls, ugly heatmap — with only 3-5 trusted people. They share back. Brutal honesty club.
features.notes: Instagram Close Friends but for shame. Tight trust circle.

features.name: Dual Streaks With Friends (🔥)
features.description: Both you and friend hit daily focus goal = '🔥 day 47 with Sarah'. Breaks if either misses. Snapchat retention mechanic repurposed.
features.notes: Top-3 highest-leverage social feature. Snapchat's entire retention engine, but for the right thing. Cheap to build.

features.name: Streak Charms (Auto-Generated Friendship Facts)
features.description: 'You and Sarah have both woken up before 7am 89 times.' Auto-surfaced facts about your relationship's data.
features.notes: Snapchat Charms equivalent. Surprising auto-insights delight.

features.name: Focus Map
features.description: Snap Map equivalent. Opt-in location share showing friends in deep work right now. 'Coffee shop: 3 friends focusing here.' Drives real-world co-work.
features.notes: Location-share is sensitive. Default off, granular privacy, optional fuzzing to neighbourhood-level.

features.name: Live Focus Rooms (Audio)
features.description: Discord-style audio rooms. 2-50 people working silently together. Mic muted, camera optional. Lo-fi study group, but verified focus state.
features.notes: WebRTC infrastructure. Could be partner-integrated rather than self-built.

features.name: Push Reactions to Friend's Failure
features.description: When friend opens TikTok 10th time, your phone buzzes. One-tap reactions: 👀 🚨 💪 ❤️ 'I'm here.' They feel watched.
features.notes: Top-3 highest-leverage social. Extends Shame Notification with bidirectional reactions. Snapchat's pulse-of-friends, repurposed.

features.name: Focus DMs (Queued During Deep Work)
features.description: DMs sent during recipient's focus block are queued. Sender sees 'Will deliver at 11:00 — they're in deep work.' Forces async, protects focus.
features.notes: DMs that respect attention. Differentiator vs. every other chat app.

features.name: Focus Aura Filter (AR)
features.description: Selfie AR filter overlays a glow based on real data. Gold = thriving. Murky = drowning. Shareable to IG/Snap = recruits new users.
features.notes: Off-platform virality. ARCore / MLKit face tracking.

features.name: Brutal Mirror Filter (AR)
features.description: AR filter re-skins your face based on actual data. Disciplined = sharp/young. Drowning = bloated/aged/foggy. Most-shared and most-controversial.
features.notes: Launch-press feature. Walks a body-image-harm line — needs careful UX + opt-in + reversibility. Pair with SAFETY-001.

features.name: Public Pre-Commitments
features.description: Post tomorrow's goals to feed. App auto-grades pass/fail at end of day. Whole network sees the outcome.
features.notes: Stickk.com pre-commitment, but social. High accountability.

features.name: Focus Duels
features.description: Challenge friend to a 2-hour focus block. Higher score wins. Loser owes a coffee / emoji / Venmo. Live scoreboard during duel.
features.notes: Light competition layer. Optional money stake plugs into FINANCE-001.

features.name: Time Donation
features.description: Gift your saved time to a friend's bank. 'Sending 1.5hr to Sarah, she's stuck on her novel.' Venmo for hours.
features.notes: Generosity mechanic. Symbolic + practical (extends partner's allowed app budget for the day).

features.name: Quiet Brag Post
features.description: Finish today's One Thing → app prompts 'share?' → one-tap post to friends. They send 🙏. Low-friction validation.
features.notes: Smallest social action with highest emotional payoff. Tiny dopamine for the right behavior.

features.name: Late Night Crew
features.description: Auto-formed group of grinders/insomniacs in your timezone, visible only 11pm-4am. Different vibe, different rules.
features.notes: Solidarity for the obsessed. Careful: must not encourage harmful sleep deprivation.

features.name: Mantra Mentions
features.description: Tag a friend in your mantra; they get a 'Risky did their mantra and mentioned you' push. Honor-by-proxy.
features.notes: Surprising emotional weight from a tiny mechanism.

features.name: Best Friends List (Top 8)
features.description: Auto-curated list of 8 most-supportive friends based on reactions sent, time donated, vouches given. Visible on profile.
features.notes: Snapchat Best Friends — friendship-as-data. Drives reciprocal supportive behavior.

features.name: Public Phone Funeral
features.description: When user beats a distraction app (30 days zero opens), trigger a community ritual. Friends 'attend' the funeral. Gravestone goes on profile.
features.notes: Memorable celebration. Extends PSYCH-014 (App Tombstones) into a social event.

features.name: HealthConnect Integration
features.description: Read all body data (steps, sleep, HR, HRV, workouts, weight, cycle, mindfulness minutes) from Android HealthConnect with permissioned scopes.
features.notes: Foundation for all body tracking. Android's permissioned health aggregator. Replaces dead Google Fit. iOS equivalent: HealthKit (v2).

features.name: Plaid / Banking Integration
features.description: OAuth bank link via Plaid (US) / TrueLayer (EU) / Tink. Pulls transactions, balances, recurring charges. Auto-categorized.
features.notes: Foundation for all finance features. Plaid charges per-connected-account. Privacy: never store credentials; bank tokens E2EE.

features.name: Strava / Garmin / Whoop / Oura Sync
features.description: OAuth integration with major fitness platforms. Pulls workouts, recovery scores, HRV, runs/rides.
features.notes: Mostly redundant with HealthConnect but Strava/Whoop have richer data (segments, recovery model). User picks which sources are authoritative.

features.name: Spotify + Last.fm Music Sync
features.description: Scrobble listening history. Powers 'top artist this week' widget and Built Wrapped — Music edition.
features.notes: Spotify OAuth + Web API. Last.fm as fallback aggregator for cross-platform music. Adds richness to year-in-review.

features.name: Letterboxd + Trakt + TV Time Sync
features.description: Pull films and TV episodes watched. Feeds 'cultural input' stats and year-in-review.
features.notes: Letterboxd has read-only API. Trakt is the cross-platform TV scrobbler standard.

features.name: Photos Library Integration
features.description: Read-only access to phone Photos library. Extract faces, places, dates. Powers Memories, People-Met Graph, Travel Atlas.
features.notes: READ_MEDIA_IMAGES + MediaStore. Faces via on-device MLKit. Privacy-critical: never upload, only read metadata + on-device inference.

features.name: Calendar Deep Integration
features.description: Beyond RESTRICT-010's time-blocking, pull all events for People-Met inference, focus-block detection, life-time-spent analytics.
features.notes: Extends RESTRICT-010 (Context-Aware Locks). Same permission, more analytical use.

features.name: GitHub / Code Output Integration
features.description: Pull commits, PRs, contribution heatmap. Feeds Output stats and Things Made counter for developers.
features.notes: GitHub OAuth + GraphQL API. Optional, developer-segment feature. Also GitLab/Bitbucket support.

features.name: Goodreads / Kindle / Audible Reading Sync
features.description: Books finished, reading minutes, highlights. Replaces manual log.
features.notes: Goodreads API is deprecated; rely on StoryGraph / Hardcover / Readwise instead. Kindle uses 'My Clippings.txt' export.

features.name: Voice-to-Structured-Data Capture
features.description: 30-sec voice note → on-device LLM extracts structured data (workout, mood, meal, expense, gratitude) and files it. Replaces typing for manual logs.
features.notes: Critical. Manual entry decays in 30 days; voice is the only sustainable manual input. On-device LLM (Gemma Nano or Phi-3) for privacy.

features.name: Daily Activity Loop
features.description: Steps, active minutes, calories, floors. Closes Apple-rings-style daily goals. Home-screen widget.
features.notes: Native rings UX. Source: HealthConnect.

features.name: Workout Log
features.description: Strength sets/reps + cardio sessions, auto-imported from Strava/Hevy/Garmin. Manual fallback via voice.
features.notes: We aggregate, don't replace, dedicated lifting apps.

features.name: PR Wall (Lifetime Records)
features.description: Lifetime personal records auto-detected from workout log. Pinned to public profile.
features.notes: Quiet flex. Detect PR via 1RM calc + cardio time-trial detection.

features.name: Sport-Specific Clocks
features.description: Time-since-last-run, days-since-rest, weeks-since-deload. Pattern detection for overtraining.
features.notes: Cheap calc, high signal for serious athletes.

features.name: Recovery Score (Synthesized)
features.description: Daily 0-100 recovery from HRV + sleep duration + sleep quality + soreness ping + RHR. Pitched at non-athletes (Whoop is for athletes only).
features.notes: Gap from research: 'recovery score for non-athletes' is unsolved (Athlytic closest). Big wedge.

features.name: Reps-as-Currency
features.description: Completed workouts mint time-credits for distraction apps. Earn entertainment with effort.
features.notes: Extends Focus Score Economy. Physical effort earns digital permission.

features.name: Form Check Video
features.description: 10-sec lift video → on-device pose-estimation critique. Optional AR overlay shows joint angles.
features.notes: MoveNet / BlazePose. Form coaching is a real unmet need at consumer level.

features.name: Meditation Session Log
features.description: Minutes, technique (breath/body-scan/mantra), guided vs unguided. HRV before/after for objective effect.
features.notes: Bring-your-own-meditation (Insight Timer / Calm / Headspace sync). We track, don't compete.

features.name: Stress Weather Report
features.description: HRV + unlock-frequency + typing cadence + voice pitch → daily 'Stormy / Cloudy / Clear' stress forecast.
features.notes: Multi-signal proxy for stress. Useful even without watch. Feeds SAFETY-001.

features.name: Voice-Tone EQ
features.description: Weekly recording of a fixed sentence. AI tracks pitch/jitter/shimmer over months. Depression / anxiety / vitality proxies nobody else surfaces.
features.notes: Real signal — vocal acoustic markers correlate with depression/Parkinson's in research. Local-only. Opt-in.

features.name: Subconscious Dump
features.description: 60-sec stream-of-consciousness voice recording before bed. On-device LLM extracts themes weekly. 'You said "tired" 47 times last week.'
features.notes: Differentiator no app has nailed. Voice-only ritual, never transcribed to screen.

features.name: Cognitive Vitals
features.description: 30-sec daily test (n-back, reaction time, Stroop). Long-term cognition curve. Early dementia red flag for the obsessed.
features.notes: Real research-grade tasks. Lumosity-but-honest. Opt-in for self-experimenters.

features.name: Dream Journal + AI Symbols
features.description: Voice-record morning recall. AI extracts recurring symbols/themes weekly. Yearly 'dreamscape' recap.
features.notes: Lucid-dreaming category abandoned since 2014. Wide-open lane.

features.name: Reading-as-Thinking Loop
features.description: Pull highlights from Readwise / Kindle. Surface a daily highlight. Tie to journal: 'this idea hit you, what do you do with it?'
features.notes: Massive gap: Goodreads tracks that you read, not what you understood. Readwise is closest but isolated.

features.name: Plate-Photo Nutrition Log
features.description: Photo a meal → on-device CV estimates calories + macros. Cal-AI-style but local.
features.notes: Cal AI proved this works. Critical: on-device model for privacy. Accuracy ~±20%.

features.name: Fasting Window Auto-Detect
features.description: Last-meal-to-first-meal timestamps auto-computed. No manual fast timer needed.
features.notes: Zero won the market with manual timers. Auto-detect is the next step.

features.name: Trigger-Food Detector
features.description: Correlate specific foods with next-day mood/energy/sleep. 'Beer is costing you 1.4 sleep points.'
features.notes: The math kills habits. Bearable does this for symptoms — apply to food.

features.name: Cooking Ratio
features.description: Meals cooked vs eaten out, with cost delta computed via finance integration. 'You ate out 14 times this month, $342 vs $89 if cooked.'
features.notes: Cross-domain (nutrition × finance) insight. Surprising to most users.

features.name: Sleep Stats Dashboard
features.description: Duration, debt, consistency, stages from HealthConnect.
features.notes: Native widget. Pairs with RESTRICT-011 Dream Mode.

features.name: Sleep ↔ Everything Correlator
features.description: 'You average 4.2 sleep score after Sundays you scrolled 3+ hrs.' Cross-correlation surface.
features.notes: Most valuable single insight engine. Sleep responds to everything; nobody surfaces it across silos.

features.name: Sleep Window Guardrails
features.description: Hard cutoff time enforced. Phone goes Dream Mode (RESTRICT-011). Won't reopen entertainment until morning alarm.
features.notes: Extends Dream Mode with biometric trigger ('you should be asleep by your usual time').

features.name: Auto-Categorized Spending
features.description: Plaid-fed transactions auto-categorized via ML. Manual override per transaction with learning.
features.notes: Copilot leads on this. ML-first vs rule-based.

features.name: Net Worth Tracker
features.description: Assets minus liabilities, line chart over time. Includes cash, investments, crypto, debts.
features.notes: Empower's free moat. We aggregate Plaid + manual entries for alt assets.

features.name: Subscription Hunter
features.description: Detects recurring charges. '$340/mo on subs you forgot.' One-tap cancel link where supported.
features.notes: Instant ROI — pays for the app. Rocket Money proved this monetizes.

features.name: Savings Rate %
features.description: Income minus spend, as % of income, per month. The only finance number that really matters.
features.notes: Single most important metric for financial health. Should be a widget.

features.name: Spend Heatmap by Place
features.description: Every dollar pinned to a geo-coordinate. See your $200/wk coffee habit as a constellation around your office.
features.notes: Cross-domain (finance × location) unlock. Nobody does this.

features.name: Time-Money Conversion
features.description: Every purchase shown as 'X hrs of your life at your hourly rate'. Extends PSYCH-007.
features.notes: User sets hourly rate once. Friction at point of swipe.

features.name: Future-Self Budget Projector
features.description: Project age-65 net worth based on current trajectory. Slider simulates 'what if I save $200 more/mo'. Devastating math.
features.notes: Empower's secret-sauce-equivalent. Compound interest visualized.

features.name: Regret Receipts
features.description: 24hr after a purchase, app asks 'still glad?' Yes/No. Builds personal regret-rate by category over time.
features.notes: Behavioral feedback loop nobody builds. Surprisingly powerful self-knowledge.

features.name: Money Mirror Tile
features.description: Home screen tile showing today's spend in real-time + estimated days-of-freedom you've gained or lost.
features.notes: Glanceable. Friction-at-point-of-swipe applied to money.

features.name: Tax-Aware Income Tracker
features.description: Every gig / 1099 / sale logged with auto-saved tax %. Quarterly nag for estimated payments. Removes April panic.
features.notes: Keeper Tax + Stride do this for gig workers. Useful side-hustle wedge.

features.name: Couples Joint Finance Mode
features.description: Shared 'we' ledger overlaying both partners' data. Pick what to share. Number-one couples fight, ended.
features.notes: Honeydue/Zeta dominate by default — bad UX. Real wedge if executed well.

features.name: Built Wrapped — Money Edition
features.description: Annual recap of finances: total earned, total saved, top categories, top regret purchase, savings rate, net-worth delta. Shareable.
features.notes: Spotify Wrapped for money is unbuilt. Massive viral surface — January is the moment.

features.name: Lifetime Location Heatmap
features.description: Every minute of life pinned on a map. Stored on-device by default. Beautiful zoomable artifact.
features.notes: Fused location provider, low-frequency sample. ~30MB/year. Privacy: on-device-only by default. Inspired by Arc.

features.name: Time-Per-Place Ranking
features.description: 'Home: 14hr/day. Office: 6hr. Coffee shop X: 3hr/week. Gym: 4hr/week.' Auto-clustered.
features.notes: DBSCAN-style clustering on location history. Auto-labels via Google Places API or user-tagged.

features.name: Third-Place Tracker
features.description: Count of distinct non-home/work places visited ≥30min/week. Loneliness leading indicator.
features.notes: Sociological research: number of 'third places' (cafés, parks, gyms) is a strong predictor of well-being.

features.name: Commute Tax Counter
features.description: Hours/year lost to commute, equivalent at hourly rate, equivalent books-not-read, gym-sessions-skipped.
features.notes: One-tile life-cost insight. Often a life-decision-changing number.

features.name: Outdoor Minutes + Nature Score
features.description: GPS + step + light sensor → 'you spent 18min outside today, average human needs 90.' Nature Score tags locations with green-space % from OSM.
features.notes: Mental-health predictor. Apple Watch has 'Time in Daylight' — we expand to nature/green-space coverage.

features.name: Travel Atlas
features.description: Every country/state/city visited with days spent each. Lifetime travel map. Beautiful on profile.
features.notes: Polarsteps proved this is shareable. Built-in to our location data.

features.name: Sound-Fingerprint Place ID
features.description: Ambient mic identifies which café/place you're in by acoustics, not GPS. Privacy-friendly indoor positioning.
features.notes: Research-grade approach. Real possibility (acoustic fingerprinting works). Optional fallback when GPS off.

features.name: Air-Quality / UV Exposure Ledger
features.description: Cross location history with AQI + UV APIs → annual μg PM2.5 inhaled, lifetime sun-on-skin minutes.
features.notes: Real health data nobody surfaces. Vitamin D + melanoma + asthma all flow from this.

features.name: Hangover Calculus
features.description: Drinks logged → next-day HRV/sleep/mood deltas charted. The math kills habits.
features.notes: Cross-correlation that no drinking app surfaces clearly. Sunnyside is closest.

features.name: Substance Log (Private)
features.description: Cannabis, mushrooms, MDMA, etc. PII-grade encryption, never synced. For therapy / harm reduction.
features.notes: Sensitive. Strict on-device. Optional therapist-share export.

features.name: Caffeine Half-Life Graph
features.description: Last coffee at 3pm = 25% still in your bloodstream at midnight. Pairs with sleep tracking.
features.notes: Trivial math, huge insight.

features.name: Time-With-People Auto-Detect
features.description: Bluetooth proximity to known devices + calendar events → time spent with each person. Auto-logged.
features.notes: Privacy-sensitive. Opt-in. Bluetooth proximity is the hardest signal to gather cleanly.

features.name: Last-Contacted List
features.description: Humans you haven't spoken to in 30/60/90 days, with nudges to reach out.
features.notes: Source: call log + message frequency + calendar. Pulled on-device only.

features.name: Reciprocity Score per Friend
features.description: Who initiates calls/texts; balance of effort over time. 'You called Sarah 12x. She called you 1x.'
features.notes: Brutal but real signal. No one surfaces it.

features.name: Family Time Budget
features.description: Weekly hours with each immediate family member, target vs actual.
features.notes: Pairs with COUPLES-001 / FAMILY-001.

features.name: People-Met Graph (Lifetime)
features.description: Lifetime graph of who you've ever shared a calendar event or photo with, when, where. Ambient PRM, no manual entry.
features.notes: Big gap in the market — nobody has built this. Privacy: on-device face recognition + calendar attendees. Powerful + sensitive.

features.name: Personal Bloomberg Terminal
features.description: Single dashboard, all dimensions side-by-side: body / mind / money / time / relationships / output. User picks widgets.
features.notes: The unified dashboard. Not the primary product, but the optional power-user view.

features.name: Auto-Insight Engine
features.description: On-device LLM scans all dimensions weekly for cross-correlations. 'On weeks you slept >7.5hr, you shipped 2.3x more code.'
features.notes: **Highest-leverage feature in the spec.** The 'mental ↔ physical correlation' nobody has built. Real wedge.

features.name: Sunday Life Review
features.description: 30-min weekly ritual. App walks you through every dimension, you reflect, set next week's One Thing.
features.notes: The actual product loop that makes everything cohere. Pairs with PROD-012 Shutdown Ritual.

features.name: Quarterly Self-Audit
features.description: 90-day review, scored, with deltas. The comp report for the company-of-one.
features.notes: Quarterly cadence catches things weekly reviews miss.

features.name: Personal Annual Report (PDF)
features.description: Year-end PDF, beautiful, exportable, shareable with therapist/spouse/coach. Like a public company files annually — but for your life.
features.notes: The shareable artifact. Print-on-demand bookable via MERCH-001.

features.name: Built Wrapped — Life Edition (Multi-Domain)
features.description: Spotify-Wrapped for your entire life: films + books + travel + people + photos + mood + money + restaurants. The single biggest viral surface.
features.notes: Multi-domain year-in-review doesn't exist in the market. We have all the data. Top-3 highest-leverage feature in spec.

features.name: Counterfactual Twin
features.description: AI persona of 'you, but with one habit different'. Show 'Sober Risky' / 'Sleeps-8hrs Risky' / 'No-Scroll Risky' trajectory next to actual you.
features.notes: Launch-press feature nobody else has. Counterfactual reasoning grounded in YOUR real data trajectory.

features.name: Digital Twin Timeline
features.description: Every datapoint of your life on one infinite scrollable timeline. Zoom into any minute of any day. Year-end animated zoom-out.
features.notes: Hard to build well. Ambitious. The 'OS for life' visualization moment.

features.name: Beach Days Remaining Widget
features.description: Actuarial life expectancy − age × proportion of summer Saturdays remaining = 'you have ~672 beach Saturdays left.' Lock-screen widget.
features.notes: Single most viral widget concept in the spec. Devastating, motivating, real. Opt-in (some find it morbid).

features.name: Compounding Curve Visualizer
features.description: Pick a habit + % improvement → see the curve over years. The Jeff-Bezos chart, personalized.
features.notes: Atomic Habits math made visual. 1% better/day = 37x in a year.

features.name: AI Life Coach (Trained on YOUR Data)
features.description: On-device LLM acts as Stoic / CBT therapist / coach. Reads your full data weekly. Asks Socratic questions. Not generic chatbot — your data informs every reply.
features.notes: Strict on-device. The differentiator vs ChatGPT-style life coaches: actual personal data, never leaves device.

features.name: Death Clock Widget (Opt-In)
features.description: Actuarial life expectancy countdown on home screen. WeCroak-style daily mortality reminder.
features.notes: WeCroak ($2 one-time) proved the demand. Pair with SAFETY-001 (suppress during crisis state).

features.name: Reading Stats
features.description: Books finished, reading minutes, top authors, pages/day. From Kindle / Audible / Readwise.
features.notes: Goodreads is stale; StoryGraph is indie. We aggregate.

features.name: Daily Highlight Resurfacing
features.description: Surface a random past highlight every morning. Spaced repetition for ideas.
features.notes: Readwise's killer feature, baked-in. With on-device LLM, can ask: 'what would you do with this idea today?'

features.name: Vocabulary Growth Curve
features.description: New words encountered + actively used in journals, tracked over months.
features.notes: Nerdy but delightful. Tied to journal text + reading highlights.

features.name: Daily Selfie Timelapse
features.description: 8am selfie every day → year-end timelapse video. Wrinkles, weight, mood visible.
features.notes: 1SE-style annual artifact. Differentiator: paired with body/mood data for context.

features.name: Promise Kept Ratio
features.description: Every commitment ('I'll do X by Friday') tagged → auto-graded at deadline. Self-trust score over time.
features.notes: Internal-honesty mechanic. Voice capture for friction-free logging.

features.name: Rejection Counter
features.description: Jia-Jiang-style. Asks you made, asks-that-got-rejected. Courage practice. 'You asked 4 times this week. 3 rejected. 1 yes.'
features.notes: Cult Jia-Jiang practice. Reframes rejection as desirable.

features.name: Risks-Taken Log
features.description: Uncomfortable conversation, big email sent, raise asked for, applied for X. Tagged at end-of-day.
features.notes: Counters comfort-zone drift. Surprisingly clarifying.

features.name: Things-Made Counter
features.description: Photos taken, videos shot, recipes cooked, drawings, songs, blog posts, social posts shipped. The verb 'made', tracked.
features.notes: Output-as-identity. Extends PROD-011 (Input/Output ratio) into concrete units.
